content: Go AES-256-GCM: nonce must be unique for all time per key. Never use more than 2^32 random nonces with a given key (collision risk). Nonce reuse is catastrophic — breaks confidentiality AND authenticity. NewGCMWithRandomNonce (Go 1.24+) auto-generates 96-bit nonce but same 2^32 message limit. GHASH not constant-time without hardware AES. Key custody is the real risk: if AES key sits on same host as ciphertext, DB breach = key breach. Envelope encryption (DEK+KEK) + key rotation is standard for self-hosted.
